name: Mercury
description: The smallest planet in our solar system and closest to the Sun, Mercury is a rocky world with extreme temperature swings and no atmosphere to speak of.
funFacts: A year on Mercury is just 88 Earth days.
Despite being closest to the Sun, it is not the hottest planet.
Mercury has no atmosphere to retain heat.

name: Venus
description: Venus is the hottest planet in the solar system, shrouded in thick clouds of sulfuric acid. Its surface is scorching and its atmospheric pressure crushes like the deep ocean.
funFacts: Venus rotates backwards compared to most planets.
A day on Venus is longer than its year.
Surface pressure is 90 times that of Earth.

name: Earth
description: Our home planet is the only known world to harbor life. With liquid water, a protective atmosphere, and a magnetic field, Earth is uniquely suited for complex life.
funFacts: 71% of Earth's surface is covered in water.
Earth is the densest planet in the solar system.
Earth's atmosphere protects us from harmful solar radiation.

name: Mars
description: Known as the Red Planet due to iron oxide on its surface, Mars hosts the tallest volcano and longest canyon in the solar system. It is the most explored planet after Earth.
funFacts: Olympus Mons on Mars is the tallest volcano in the solar system.
Mars has two small moons: Phobos and Deimos.
A Martian day is only 37 minutes longer than an Earth day.

name: Jupiter
description: The king of planets, Jupiter is a gas giant so massive it could swallow all other planets combined. Its iconic Great Red Spot is a centuries-old storm larger than Earth.
funFacts: Jupiter's Great Red Spot is a storm older than 350 years.
Jupiter has the shortest day of all planets at just 10 hours.
More than 1,300 Earths could fit inside Jupiter.

name: Saturn
description: Saturn's breathtaking ring system makes it one of the most recognizable planets. Despite its size, it is the least dense planet — less dense than water.
funFacts: Saturn is the least dense planet and could float on water.
Its rings are made of ice and rock particles.
Saturn has 146 known moons, including Titan which has a thick atmosphere.

name: Uranus
description: An ice giant tilted on its side, Uranus rotates nearly perpendicular to its orbit. Its cyan color comes from methane in its atmosphere absorbing red light.
funFacts: Uranus rotates at a 98 degree tilt, essentially on its side.
It is the coldest planetary atmosphere in the solar system.
Uranus has 13 known rings.

name: Neptune
description: The windiest planet in the solar system, Neptune's storms rage at supersonic speeds. This deep blue ice giant was the first planet discovered through mathematical prediction.
funFacts: Winds on Neptune can reach 2,100 km/h, the fastest in the solar system.
Neptune was the first planet predicted mathematically before being observed.
One year on Neptune equals 165 Earth years.

name: Pluto
description: Once the ninth planet, Pluto was reclassified as a dwarf planet in 2006. It has a heart-shaped nitrogen ice plain and a surprisingly complex surface for such a small world.
funFacts: Pluto was considered the 9th planet for 76 years.
Pluto's largest moon Charon is half the size of Pluto itself.
A day on Pluto lasts about 6.4 Earth days.

name: The Sun
description: The Sun is the star at the heart of our solar system — a massive sphere of hot plasma generating energy through nuclear fusion. It accounts for 99.86% of the total mass of the solar system.
funFacts: The Sun contains 99.86% of the mass of the entire solar system.
Light from the Sun takes about 8 minutes to reach Earth.
The Sun is about 4.6 billion years old and halfway through its life.
